full_24h_all_signals:
(U) BLUF  
RF activity at Camp Pendleton main gate (San Onofre) during 2026-04-26 00:00Z to 2026-04-26 23:59Z is dominated by expected gate, transit, chow, and office-hour patterns. Primary concern is Cluster 6 along the east/west perimeter fence, where unidentified, locally-administered and ESP32-associated signatures show a nighttime-only bias inconsistent with normal fixed infrastructure.

(U) Target / Location Summary  
Survey covered the Camp Pendleton main gate perimeter for the full 24-hour scan window. Pattern of life reflects normal installation activity: nightly persistent dwell at the gate shack (Cluster 1), a midday congregation at the chow hall (Cluster 2), commuter movement on the main road (Cluster 3), office-hour fixed Wi-Fi at Bldg 22 (Cluster 4), and office-hour IoT/sensor activity at Bldg 44 motor pool (Cluster 5). The only notable irregularity is Cluster 6, an ephemeral signature set distributed along the perimeter fence east/west with sporadic activity and a nightly bias.

(U) Device Counts by Class  
Total collection was 5,000 RF events comprising 2,700 Wi-Fi and 2,300 Bluetooth observations, representing 1,180 unique MACs. By cluster/device class, observed concentrations were: Cluster 1 (880) Wi-Fi AP + phone + beacon; Cluster 2 (1,090) phone + wearable; Cluster 3 (980) phone; Cluster 4 (650) Wi-Fi AP; Cluster 5 (510) IoT + sensor; and Cluster 6 (490) unknown OUI devices.

(U) Suspicious or Anomalous Signatures  
Classifier flagged Cluster 6 for 220 events using locally-administered MAC prefixes (02:00:00 / 06:11:22) with no vendor attribution, concentrated along east and west perimeter fences and weighted toward 0200-0500 local. Additionally, three Espressif (ESP32) MACs were detected on the western fence at RSSI -82 to -94 with no daytime presence, consistent with battery-powered drop sensors. These signatures differ from the stable fixed-infrastructure behavior seen in Clusters 4 and 5 and from the routine human activity in Clusters 1-3.

(U) Recommended ISR Follow-ups  
Prioritize focused nighttime ISR on Cluster 6 fence segments, especially 0200-0500 local, to correlate RF hits with physical emplacement, line-of-sight, and possible concealment points. Re-scan western fence for repeat appearance of the three ESP32 MACs, attempt directional geolocation on locally-administered emitters, and compare recurrence against normal gate traffic from Clusters 1 and 3 to rule out incidental passerby devices.

(U) Confidence  
MED — Strong confidence in baseline activity characterization; moderate confidence on threat significance because anomalous signatures are clear but attribution remains unresolved.
bt_only_workhours:
(U) BLUF  
During the 2026-04-26 09:00Z to 17:00Z scan window at Camp Pendleton main gate (San Onofre) perimeter, the RF picture was entirely Bluetooth-based and dominated by routine personnel congregation plus fixed/support infrastructure signatures. Primary concern is a persistent beacon-only population in Cluster 3 at the vehicle park, specifically 24 Estimote MACs with no associated phones, requiring verification as authorized inventory tags versus covert locators.

(U) Target / Location Summary  
Survey covered the Camp Pendleton main gate (San Onofre) perimeter and logged 1,450 total RF events from 510 unique MACs, with 0 Wi-Fi detections and 1,450 Bluetooth detections. Pattern of life was concentrated across four clusters: Cluster 1 at the chow hall showed a strong 1100-1300 gathering spike, Cluster 2 at Bldg 44 motorpool reflected office-hours fixed infrastructure activity, Cluster 3 at the vehicle park reflected afternoon-shift beacon dwell, and Cluster 4 at the gate shack showed an office-hours subset of phone and beacon dwell.

(U) Device Counts by Class  
Observed device classes were phone+wearable in Cluster 1 (n=720), iot+sensor in Cluster 2 (n=320), beacon in Cluster 3 (n=240), and phone+beacon in Cluster 4 (n=170). By event volume, the environment was led by personnel-associated devices at the chow hall, followed by fixed/support infrastructure at the motorpool, then beacon-heavy dwell signatures in the vehicle park and gate shack.

(U) Suspicious or Anomalous Signatures  
Classifier flagged one notable anomaly: within Cluster 3, 24 Estimote beacon MACs persisted in the vehicle park without any associated phone presence. This is anomalous relative to normal personal-device pairing behavior and may indicate either legitimate inventory tracking tags or unauthorized/covertly placed locators; no additional anomalies were provided for Clusters 1, 2, or 4.

(U) Recommended ISR Follow-ups  
Conduct physical verification and asset-owner coordination for the 24 Estimote MACs in Cluster 3, with emphasis on whether they map to authorized inventory control at the vehicle park. Cross-check Cluster 4 gate shack beacon/phone subset against approved gate operations equipment, and maintain repeat scans over the same 09:00Z-17:00Z window to confirm whether Cluster 3 persistence and Cluster 1 lunch-period spike remain stable.

(U) Confidence  
MED — confidence is supported by strong event volume and coherent clustering across the full scan window, but limited by Bluetooth-only collection and no corroborating Wi-Fi or external attribution data.
